title: Troubleshooting, Security, Recovery, and Operations Handoff · Operations Defense
summary: Resolve a multi-layer workstation incident and hand off a safe, reproducible operating procedure to another technician. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a complete Linux operations evidence portfolio. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Form competing hypotheses, select discriminating observations, and update confidence without changing multiple layers at once.
Use privilege only for the smallest justified operation while preserving command provenance, environment boundaries, and audit evidence.
Preview scope, snapshot relevant state, define stop conditions, and test rollback before risky filesystem, package, or service changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Hypothesis Ladder · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 5.

Which action demonstrates Hypothesis Ladder without dropping earlier contracts?

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Hypothesis Ladder evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Hypothesis Ladder assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A quiz Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 5.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Hypothesis Ladder has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

options.text: Capture user, directory, environment, target state, and authoritative command behavior
Predict shell parsing, expansion, permissions, side effects, exit status, and safety boundary
Run the smallest simulated observation or reversible change and retain exact output
Verify changed state, failure path, rollback, and an accessible operations handoff

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Operations Handoff: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Linux incident crosses filesystem, permission, process, service, and network evidence. Preserve a baseline, change one bounded layer, record exit status, and verify rollback on case 2.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Write an accessible runbook with prerequisites, exact observations, safe actions, expected signals, exceptions, recovery, and ownership.
